material_claims.claim: The captured PIP-1 through PIP-3 instruments support distinct roles for the POLIS electorate, Star Atlas Foundation, and Star Atlas Council.

The DAO exercises proposal-specific PVP-weighted voting authority; the Foundation supplies the described legal and administrative capacity; the Council serves as an elected process steward with delegated duties.
Operational DAO release statements in 2022, public PIP design communications in 2023, and formal proposal votes in 2024 are distinct milestones and may coexist in the constitutional history.
The Council is an independent legislative sovereign or the Foundation is the DAO electorate.
material_claims.reason: The captured instruments divide voting, administration, and process-steward responsibilities rather than merging the institutions.

material_claims.claim: Passage of PIP-1, PIP-2, or PIP-3 proves every later implementation, delegation, payment, or current officeholder.
material_claims.reason: Proposal passage establishes authorization; later institutional acts require their own dated evidence.

known_limitations: The 2023 PIP-1 publication has not been textually reconciled with the 2024 captured proposal.
Current Foundation delegations, Council membership, and governance-program deployment require later dated records.
The completed-binary vote rule is a repository editorial adjudication and is not asserted as source-native PIP-1 text.
